summary: Unclear weight between strategic housing need and local restrictive criteria leads to inconsistent refusals and appeals.
Validation requirements differ materially between LPAs, creating repeat submissions and front-end delay.
Local plan and supplementary guidance repeat overlapping design tests with slight wording differences, causing officer interpretation drift.
Environmental assessment sequencing often duplicates evidence requests across regimes, extending determination timelines.
Transitional policy periods create uncertainty where emerging local plans are given differing weight across authorities.
